page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a bundled local storage capacity of 5700 GB delivered via NVMe SSDs. Network performance is supported by a 40 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core compute and memory subsystem performance. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, registering top-tier results for both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached read/write operations. Database operations, including PassMark database tests and Redis SET operations, also rank in the top 10%, alongside static web serving throughput. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core results in the middle 50% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture and high resource density of the Graviton processor deliver strong performance-per-watt and resource allocation without hyperthreading overhead. However, the lack of hardware-accelerated GPUs limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, although it demonstrates strong text generation speeds for medium and large language models. The platform is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale relational and non-relational databases, caching layers, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture operating at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, including Redis SET throughput. While single-core performance is moderate, the high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated physical cores offer excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput web serving, caching layers, and large-scale data processing tasks that benefit from local high-speed storage and high memory capacity without hyperthreading overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server with 96 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier multi-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density, bare-metal ARMv9 server combining massive DDR5 memory capacity with high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated physical ARMv9 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
5700 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving
